concerns.text: FR-008 mandates '≥95% continuous monthly alignment' while US-1 and FR-001/002 explicitly require 'weekly frequency' alignment. The unit of time (monthly vs weekly) is contradictory, creating an impossible constraint for a weekly-aligned dataset.

SC-006 references FR-008 and FR-010 for validation. FR-008 (monthly alignment) contradicts the weekly scope of the project. Additionally, SC-006 validates 'sensitivity analysis' (US-3) against FR-008 (data ingestion) and FR-010 (sample size flagging), which are distinct functional areas, creating a traceability mismatch.

Acceptance Scenario 3 states '5–[deferred] of data is randomly masked'. The syntax '5–[deferred]' is ambiguous. It implies a range starting at 5% but ending at an undefined value. The spec should clarify if the upper bound is also deferred or if '5%' is the fixed lower bound for the sensitivity test.

FR-010 states the system must 'exclude' periods if low-confidence data exceeds 10%. However, US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2 only requires such periods to be 'flagged and excluded' if the missing rate exceeds 5%. The 10% threshold in FR-010 conflicts with the 5% threshold in US-1 for the same condition (missing/low-confidence data handling).
